item.title: Portal & SCP Lies
item.content:encoded: Window & cake is a lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like there's some unsubtle commentary in here, and I'm iffy on the containment procedures, but overall it kept my interest the entire time and had some great imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's just there for if they do testing, this is what they're supposed to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've got a feeling the issues are artifacts of the article being updated over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused as well. Nothing in the mail body or the addendum seems to indicate why they need to be unclothed or "fed" to it.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I enjoyed reading the SCP, I am going to have to say -1. This feels a little rushed, or not thought through. With the sacrifice, the code refreshing, no real testing or effects, and what felt like lack of interest on the creators part.

With this being said, I would like to see some testing done or read about some results from tests. That may give some in sight as to why the D class are being sacrificed. Also how did they find out that D class should go in naked, did this happen once, and there is a set of cloths forever on the floor as a reminder.

Again while I did enjoy this read, it just did not resonate with me like others have. If there are changes done, I would enjoy coming back to check this one out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this. At first glance it sort of seems like one of those SCPs where we have an item that is nothing but a benefit to humanity, but there is an underlying sense of mystery and dread to this. Almost like the fly, smelling the sweet nectar in the mouth of the flytrap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why are D-class thrown at it to die when it doesn't need feeding?
Why can't the assigned guard open the chamber remotely from a room they are locked in for the duration of the test to minimize exposure and risk?

Did I miss something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea behind this quite a bit, but there's a few things that either bother me and another few things I think could be added or changed.

Is Director Hussein's addenda intentionally over-dramatic as a result of exposure to the SCP? It kinda feels like that's the intention but it isn't very clear.

As this SCP is a cognitohazard very blatantly inspired by vaporwave, it would be interesting to further explore its psychological effect on subjects. Perhaps expand on the Director's exposure effects through an increased appreciation of aesthetics (unnecessary renovations to the reliquary site?) as well as an increased affinity for consumerism (installing additional TVs and vending machines?). Causing people to behave "under the influence" of vaporwave makes for a seemingly innocuous but actually pretty scary SCP.

A memey point could be added that extended exposure to the SCP's effects causes perception of time and perception of the broadcast music to slow down (if the latter, perhaps the music plays back normally in recordings). It wouldn't add much to the horror aspect, but would help flesh out the idea that this thing basically tranquilizes whoever is exposed to it.

The containment procedures could do with a re-write for clarification. I'm assuming the cycling codes is so that the guard can resist the compulsion to enter the chamber and be at Home(tm). However, it doesn't really make sense why D-Class are apparently offered as routine sacrifices.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the idea of a vaporwave scp, but this just doesn't work for me. I don't see any reason to be concerned about this thing or why they have to shove people at it and then instakill them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lool, I didn't intend that. But sure, why not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed this, but is this SCP implying that due to it's memetic effects, this is the reason why vaporwave exists in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great SCP. I honestly didn't catch onto it being vaporwave until I read the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes me long for a Baltic sunrise. Good job you glorious bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was the best way to incorporate vaporwave into the SCP Foundation.

Congrats. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhh…I'm not sure how to feel about this. On the one hand, I thoroughly enjoy vaporwave (in fact i own a goddamn solo cup shirt), so that makes me feel kinda bad when I say I don't think I really like this? It seems a little too…straightforward for an SCP, and so very OBVIOUSLY vaporwave, even using the done-to-death Greco-Roman bust trope. It just…I see where you're coming from, but I just think it needs a bit more development? Idk. I might read it again when it's not 3 AM.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OMG. Someone listening to vaporwave. You are my homie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand the purpose of exposing a person to it weekly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly what I thought. The Vaporwave Room. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That audio file sounds like GLaDOS from Portal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Update; both image files have been updated. Thanks very much to Petrograd did a much better job than I did of editing the main image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't believe it. Someone actually made vaporwave… into an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this vaporwave?

EDIT: Saw the filename, vaporwave confirmed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kept my interest all the way through, although the note seemed to be doing its best to lose me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough; I'm not exactly a photoshop god, that's the thing. I think I might have some window textures somewhere, so I might be able to update the sunrise image to make it seem more as if it was through a window.

I see your point about the E classification though. C seemed to fit the situation better as indicated by the Clearance Levels Guide at the time, but come to think of it, E seems to work better upon rereading.

Edit: I've done a quick edit. Does it look obvious enough as to be through a windowpane? Of course, the addendum text would have to be changed to suit what the image shows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so, I'm upvoting this. It's one of the things I like the most, and that's a skip that's memetic, not well understood, and has potentially already breached containment in ways the Foundation is not capable of recognizing or admitting.

I do have some issues with it, however:

+ show block
– hide block

Minor point: To my understanding, Foundation personnel who have to be recertified after contact with a skip are Class E, rather than C. Nobody's required to use that classification system, but that's the convention.

Bigger point, though: The images are not doing it for me. I like the bust and I think it fits very well conceptually, but the fact that the eyes were brighter before you modified them makes them look, well, really photoshopped rather than being painted pink. The sunset image is fine, but the text concerning it is a little odd. The researcher's strong emphasis on how crystal clear the glass is (while referencing the image, no less) almost comes off as a justification for the image file instead of shocked description of the phenomenon in question.

I think the main image would work with some more massaging, but at the moment it just doesn't quite work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite nice. The addenda and the some-what creepy audio recording really added spectacularly to the article.
Also upvote because of voldermort-looking bust too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeahhh, I'll give a +1. The writing in the addendum is a little off, like the researcher enjoys being florid in his reports, but I like the idea that the sense of reward was palpably forced. Far too often "it makes you feel stuff" is left as just that. I also liked the trademarks in the audio portion. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Phew, okay. Thank god the files for this page worked out.

The files included are:

  • A photoshopped version of "Galtes color terra" by Flickr user Carquinyol, under appropriate creative commons licensing. I think the bust is, in fact, of Catalonian origin.
  • A cropped version of "sunrise baltic sea" by Flickr user elbfoto, under appropriate creative commons licensing.
  • A self-recorded text-to-speech rendition of what I have written using a Chrome add-on entitled "Select and Speak". I don't know if I actually have to attribute this.

Thanks very much to Eskobar to in the forums for criticism on this. I've posted at least three failed articles in the past month or so, but I'm most proud of this one.
